title: Rename Taxonomy to Category Throughout the Application
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to see 'Category' instead of 'Taxonomy' in the UI, so that the terminology is familiar and doesn't require specialized knowledge.
description:
Rename all user-facing instances of "Taxonomy" to "Category" on the metrics tab and related modals. The internal system continues to use "taxonomy" but user-facing labels should say "Category" for better comprehension.

## Changes
- Metrics tab: all "Taxonomy" labels changed to "Category"
- Modal title: "Select Taxonomy" → "Select Category"
- OffCanvas screen labels updated
- Add close button (X) to the category modal upper right
- Add close button to the Metric Aliases popup

## Bug Fix
- Investigate and fix a Handsontable (#hot element) sizing issue causing the category modal to render at 100px height. A script was explicitly setting `#hot` to 100px.
acceptance_criteria: Close button (X) appears in upper right of category modal
Close button appears on Metric Aliases popup
Category modal renders at correct height without 100px constraint
Internal system references to 'taxonomy' remain unchanged
ux_changes: All user-facing 'Taxonomy' labels renamed to 'Category' across metrics tab, modals, and OffCanvas screens. Close buttons added to category and alias modals. Fixed a Handsontable sizing bug causing modal height issues.
implementation_notes: The Handsontable (#hot element) sizing bug was caused by a script explicitly setting the container height to 100px. CSS and JS fixes were applied. The modal rendering issue was difficult to reproduce consistently and may have been browser/cache related.
comment_summary: Extensive debugging of the Handsontable modal sizing issue. Developer could not reproduce the 100px height bug consistently. Ryan identified that a script was explicitly setting #hot to 100px. Multiple rounds of fixes applied, with the issue eventually resolving. Label changes were straightforward.
